notes: all combat numbers are simulator output on unverified constants
checkpoint levels are provisional (no level timeline in the match feed)
evidence deltas carry finished-inventory survivorship bias
the sim opens on Spectral Schematics, which the field rarely builds (1% pick) — a sim-only call; compare the meta cores below
the sim build reproduces only 33% of the field's core for Argus (it never builds Azure Core, Entropy) — some of those items ride mechanics the sim doesn't model yet

coachLine: Sim-optimal for 10s rotation vs squishies: first spike lands around minute 12 (Spectral Schematics), third item by minute 24. Take Demiurge: +13.9% on 20-second fights at minute 15.

all.name: Marrow
all.note: Marrow (Major): missing-health-dependent omnivamp; no sustain-uptime model yet

all.name: Nihil
all.note: Nihil (Major): accrual rate depends on combat uptime (how long Doom persists per fight), which the closed-form sim cannot observe

all.name: Aion
all.note: Aion (Major): economy effect; valuable but outside combat objectives (revisit with gold curves)

all.name: Exarch
all.note: Exarch (Major): buffs the HEAL TARGET (usually an ally) — team-side value outside the single-hero sim; self-heal uptime depends on heal cadence vs the 4s window

metaBuilds.title: Magical Frontline
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for physical survivability, online around minute 24 — about 3,773 effective HP vs physical in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer sees +18% more physical survivability: try Spectral Schematics over Azure Core (sim-only — the popular pick may carry utility the sim can't see; test it).

metaBuilds.title: Magical Burst
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for one-combo burst, online around minute 24 — about 691 damage in one combo in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer sees +33% more one-combo burst: try Spectral Schematics over Azure Core (sim-only — the popular pick may carry utility the sim can't see; test it).

metaBuilds.title: Magical Skirmisher
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for 10s rotation damage, online around minute 24 — about 926 damage over a 10s rotation in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer sees 2.2× the 10s rotation damage: try Spectral Schematics over Azure Core (sim-only — the popular pick may carry utility the sim can't see; test it).

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. The Fey deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Oblivion Crown: their burst combo hits 22% less hard, and this kit gives up 24% of its damage for it.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Renna's main threat is Mortal Coil; bait it before you commit, and mixed resist softens the trade.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Oblivion Crown: their burst combo hits 25% less hard, and this kit gives up 24% of its damage for it.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Iggy & Scorch deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Oblivion Crown: their burst combo hits 23% less hard, and this kit gives up 24% of its damage for it.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Gideon deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Oblivion Crown: their burst combo hits 26% less hard, and this kit gives up 24% of its damage for it.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Countess deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Oblivion Crown: their burst combo hits 20% less hard, and this kit gives up 24% of its damage for it.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Lt. Belica executes low targets with Neural Disruptor — don't linger below half HP.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Oblivion Crown: their burst combo hits 20% less hard, and this kit gives up 25% of its damage for it.

laneSteer.provenance: “Mana Catalyst” ⇒ on-hit; the sim can’t model this augment — steered by the declared playstyle + field evidence — field midlane 54.9% over 2,377 games, magnitude not simulated.

metaBuilds.title: Magical Frontline
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for physical survivability, online around minute 26 — about 5,113 effective HP vs physical in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer sees 2.2× the physical survivability: try Stonewall over Timewarp (sim-only — the popular pick may carry utility the sim can't see; test it).

metaBuilds.title: Magical Bruiser (Mixed-Def)
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for magical survivability, online around minute 27 — about 3,844 effective HP vs magical in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer sees +35% more magical survivability: try Stonewall over Timewarp (sim-only — the popular pick may carry utility the sim can't see; test it).

metaBuilds.title: Magical Burst
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for one-combo burst, online around minute 26 — about 544 damage in one combo in the sim.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Phase's main threat is Energy Lance; bait it before you commit, and mixed resist softens the trade.

They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Riktor ramps on auto-attacks — pressure them before they itemize, and mixed resist blunts them later.

Whenever this lane is not even, the winnable fight is yours: play aggressive, but respect jungle attention. Zinx's main threat is Ricochet; bait it before you commit, and mixed resist softens the trade.
They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Dekker deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.
Whenever this lane is not even, the winnable fight is yours: play aggressive, but respect jungle attention. Adele deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Unbroken Will: their burst combo hits 22% less hard, and this kit gives up 0% of its damage for it.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Mourn's main threat is Abduct; bait it before you commit, and mixed resist softens the trade.

laneSteer.provenance: “Infinite Obliteration” ⇒ poke; the sim can’t model this augment — steered by the declared playstyle + field evidence — field support 49.3% over 871 games, magnitude not simulated.

flags: crests and consumables not yet in the build model; augments with tractable mechanics are simulated, the rest are listed unmodeled
